title: centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri
intro: Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri, commonly known as Keller's centranthus or yellow centranthus, is a perennial plant in the family Valerianaceae. It is used for its medicinal properties, which include providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and other stress-related disorders, thanks to its health benefits. The therapeutic actions of Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri include sedative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to its use in herbal medicine. However, the specific bioactive constituents of this plant have not been extensively documented, but it is believed to contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its therapeutic actions.
benefits: Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri has many benefits, such as its ability to improve respiratory health by reducing inflammation and congestion, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like asthma and bronchitis. It also possesses antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing and prevent infections, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The plant's extracts have been shown to have anti-cancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, or programmed cell death, which can help prevent the progression of various types of cancer. By harnessing the potential of Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri, individuals can improve their quality of life by reducing their risk of chronic diseases and promoting a healthier, more balanced lifestyle.
constituents: Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These medicinal compounds include kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The plant's active constituents, including centranutin and centranol, also exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in the treatment of various conditions, including arthritis and autoimmune diseases. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri, individuals can benefit from improved overall well-being, enhanced immune function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
parts: Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain various bioactive compounds. The roots of this plant contain iridoids, including aucubin and catalpol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, the flowers also contain essential oils with potential antispasmodic and sedative effects.
side_effects: Centranthus longiflorus subsp. kellereri can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or flowers can also lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils may cause respiratory problems, headaches, and dizziness. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions.
